text: **Anna:** Hello, Miss {Weaver|ウェーバー先生}.
**Fanny:** Oh, Anna.
**Anna:** I've {invited|招待する|/ɪnˈvaɪtɪd/|I invited my friends to a party.} some people to dinner Saturday.
**Anna:** We're going to that new {seafood|魚介類|/ˈsiːfuːd/|I love seafood restaurants.} restaurant, fish on a dish.
**Anna:** Why don't you join us?
**Anna:** My {treat|おごり/ご馳走|/triːt/|Dinner is my treat tonight.}
**Anna:** Thanks, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver, but I already have plans.
**Anna:** My friend has invited me to her {houseboat|船に浮かぶ家|/ˈhaʊsboʊt/|She lives on a houseboat near the river.}
**Phoenix:** House boat?
**Anna:** Really?
**Anna:** That's {unique|独特の/珍しい|/juːˈniːk/|What a unique idea!}
**Anna:** Yes, it is.
**Anna:** In fact, I'm thinking of uh living on a houseboat.
**Anna:** Aren't you too tall and {klutzy|不器用な|/ˈklʌtsi/|He is very klutzy in sports.} to live on a houseboat?
**Anna:** I am not too tall.
**Anna:** Sorry.
**Anna:** Is Anna really ready for a houseboat?
**Anna:** Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver invited Anna to dinner.
**Anna:** When we make friendly, informal {invitations|招待/誘い|/ˌɪnvɪˈteɪʃənz/|We sent out invitations to the party.}, we use phrases like
**Anna:** Why don't?
**Anna:** For example, Ms.
**Anna:** Weaver said, How don't you join us?
**Anna:** My treat.
**Anna:** That's
**Anna:** Why don't?
**Anna:** Plus the subject plus the simple form of the verb.
**Anna:** Anna responded to the invitation by saying, Thanks, but I already have plans.
**Anna:** Keep watching for more examples of informal invitations.
**Fanny:** Anna, I'm so glad you could {come|来る/参加する|/kʌm/|Come and join us.}
**Anna:** Hi, Fanny.
**Fanny:** Hi, this is my son {Phoenix|不死鳥/名前|/ˈfiːnɪks/|Phoenix is a unique name.}
**Fanny:** Hi, Phoenix.
**Anna:** Hello.
**Anna:** How about you give Anna a {tour|案内/見学|/tʊər/|Let's take a tour of the museum.} and I will uh make some {lunch|昼食|/lʌntʃ/|We eat lunch at noon.}?
**Anna:** Aye, Captain.
**Fanny:** Great!
**Anna:** Do you really call her Captain?
**Anna:** So Phoenix, I think I'd like to live on a {houseboat|船に浮かぶ家|/ˈhaʊsboʊt/|We live on a houseboat near the river.}
**Anna:** Do you like living here?
**Phoenix:** I love it.
**Phoenix:** It's much {different|異なる/違う|/ˈdɪfərənt/|This is different from what I expected.} than living in a house.
**Anna:** Oh, I bet
**Anna:** I bet you had to {get rid of|処分する/捨てる|/gɛt rɪd əv/|I need to get rid of old clothes.} a lot of stuff, didn't you?
**Anna:** We moved from a three-story house to a boat, so we really had to {downsize|規模を縮小する|/ˈdaʊnˌsaɪz/|Companies often downsize during economic challenges.}
**Anna:** I'd have to get rid of a lot of stuff
**Anna:** I have a {rock collection|岩のコレクション|/rɑːk kəˈlɛkʃən/|My rock collection is very special.} and I have some really big rocks.
**Anna:** Can you stop this boat from {rocking|揺れる|/ˈrɑːkɪŋ/|The boat is rocking in the waves.} for a minute?
**Anna:** No, we're floating on water.
**Anna:** I can't do that.
**Anna:** Well, since you live on the water, aren't you afraid of {great white sharks|ホホジロザメ|/greɪt waɪt ʃɑːrks/|Great white sharks are dangerous predators.}?
**Phoenix:** No, not really.
**Phoenix:** They don't live in these waters.
**Anna:** That you know of?
**Anna:** What about {killer whales|シャチ|/ˈkɪlər weɪlz/|Killer whales are intelligent marine mammals.}?
**Phoenix:** No.
**Anna:** {Electric eels|電気ウナギ|/ɪˈlɛktrɪk iːlz/|Electric eels can generate electricity.}?
**Phoenix:** No.
**Anna:** {Barracudas|バラクーダ|/bəˈrɑːkʊdəz/|Barracudas are fast predatory fish.}?
**Phoenix:** No.
**Anna:** Piranhas?
**Anna:** Mm-mm.

**Fanny:** You know, Anna, if you're afraid of {sea life|海の生き物|/siː laɪf/|Many sea life creatures are colorful.}, maybe you shouldn't live on a {houseboat|船に浮かぶ家|/ˈhaʊsboʊt/|We enjoy summer on a houseboat.}
**Fanny:** Do you have any {boating|ボート乗り|/ˈboʊtɪŋ/|Boating is fun on weekends.} experience?
**Anna:** Oh yeah, Fanny.
**Anna:** I have {battled|戦った/対処した|/ˈbætl/|He battled the difficult problem.} the great waters before.
**Anna:** Once on a swan boat.
**Anna:** And once in a duck boat.
**Anna:** Bus.
**Anna:** It was a boat that turned into a bus.
**Fanny:** Yeah, I don't think that counts.
**Anna:** Fanny, uh, does this {rocking|揺れ/揺動|/ˈrɒkɪŋ/|The rocking chair felt comfortable.} ever bother you?
**Fanny:** No, I love it.
**Fanny:** It goes up and down and side to side and up and down, down.
**Phoenix:** I love it.
**Fanny:** Anna, are you feeling okay?
**Phoenix:** Uh yeah, yeah.
**Anna:** Uh you know, I just need some fresh air.
**Fanny:** Let me open a window for you.
**Anna:** No, I need some fresh land air.
**Anna:** Uh I mean I need to breathe air on land.
**Anna:** Uh oh, is that the time
**Anna:** I should really be going.
**Anna:** Thanks for lunch, Fanny, and a tour of your houseboat.
**Anna:** It's given me a great {idea|アイデア/考え|/aɪˈdiə/|That's a creative idea.}
**Anna:** You're gonna live on a houseboat?
**Anna:** Sort of.
**Anna:** Thanks again.
**Anna:** Hi, how would you like to {hang out|一緒に過ごす|/hæŋ aʊt/|Let's hang out this weekend.} on my boat?
**Anna:** Great, see you then
**Anna:** And this is my {shark|サメ捕獲網|shark net is used to protect swimmers.} net.
**Anna:** Thank you for the {invitation|招待|/ˌɪnvɪˈteɪʃən/|I received an invitation to the party.}, Anna.
**Anna:** Of course.
**Anna:** Would you like more {orange|オレンジ色の/オレンジジュース|/ˈɒrɪndʒ/|I like orange juice in the morning.} juice?
**Anna:** Ahoy.
